qa_pairs.question: Why is it necessary to renew Alliance accounts annually?
qa_pairs.answer: The annual renewal of all user accounts is essential in order to collect up-to-date information required by our granting agencies and to deactivate accounts that are no longer in use.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to renew their Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: All Alliance users must renew their account between May 6 and June 9, 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if an Alliance account is not renewed by the specified deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not renew your Alliance account between May 6 and June 9, 2026, your account will be automatically deactivated after that date, meaning you will not be able to access your data, but it will not be deleted immediately.

qa_pairs.question: Can a deactivated Alliance account be renewed?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you can renew your account at any time after it has been deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Do recent Alliance account holders need to renew their account immediately?
qa_pairs.answer: An exception is made for recent account holders: if you created your account after January 1, you do not have to renew it at this time.

qa_pairs.question: What must new Alliance account holders do even if they don't need to renew their account?
qa_pairs.answer: If you created your account after January 1, you still have to accept the new policies and consents, which will prompt on CCDB the first time you try to log in.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the agreements I've accepted for my Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: All agreements that you are required to accept or indicate your preference have to be accepted on CCDB. Once signed, you can see them in the Agreements page at [https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/agreements/user_index].

qa_pairs.question: What types of agreements are included on the CCDB Agreements page?
qa_pairs.answer: The agreements include Policies, Standards, Terms, and Procedures; Consent to Access User Data; Optional transfer of file ownership on account deactivation; and Consent to receive optional news and communications from the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Alliance policies?
qa_pairs.answer: The policies are available on the Alliance website at [https://alliancecan.ca/en/policies].

qa_pairs.question: What defines a primary role in an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Your primary role must correspond to your main position, generally the one you will hold for the longest.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide an example of what constitutes a primary role?
qa_pairs.answer: For example, if you have a full-time faculty appointment at one institution and adjunct appointments at other universities, your faculty appointment should be your primary role. If you are a student or a researcher, your primary role should be the one with your primary supervisor.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a user does not renew their primary role?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not renew your primary role during the annual renewal period, your account will be deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: Are new roles automatically set as primary?
qa_pairs.answer: When new roles are activated, they are automatically set as the primary role by default, but users can change their primary role at any time.

qa_pairs.question: Why are primary roles considered important?
qa_pairs.answer: Primary roles are important because they help us to manage accounts with multiple roles, facilitate communication with those users, and help with reporting.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to change a primary role in an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users with multiple active roles can change their primary role at any time on CCDB's 'Home' page.

qa_pairs.question: How can a user change their primary role on CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: To change your primary role, simply go to the role of your choosing on CCDB's 'Home' page and click on the 'Make this role primary' checkbox located next to it.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my situation, such as my faculty position or academic program, has changed?
qa_pairs.answer: If your role has changed, you must apply for a new role, which you can do by logging into CCDB and going to 'My Account' → 'Apply for a new role'.

qa_pairs.question: If I need several roles, how should I request them?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need several roles, please request one role at a time.

qa_pairs.question: What is the default setting for a newly approved role?
qa_pairs.answer: Any new role approved is set as your primary role by default.

qa_pairs.question: How can I prevent a new role from being set as my primary role?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not wish to have a new role set as your primary role, just uncheck the 'Make this role primary?' box.

qa_pairs.question: How do I deactivate old roles that are no longer needed?
qa_pairs.answer: If your current role(s) are no longer needed, please click on “disable old roles.” This will leave your new role as the primary one and deactivate any other role you have.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if my Alliance account is deactivated and I need to access my data for transfer, but I don't intend to renew the account?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca so that we can assist you with your request.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I cannot find the answer to my Alliance account renewal question in this FAQ?
qa_pairs.answer: Please send an email to renewals@tech.alliancecan.ca so that we can assist you with your request.

qa_pairs.question: As a faculty member, what happens if I fail to renew my Alliance account by the deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: Any active account that is not renewed between May 6 and June 9, 2026, will expire and be automatically deactivated after that date.

qa_pairs.question: Can a faculty member's deactivated account be reactivated?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, accounts can be reactivated at any time, even after they have been deactivated.

qa_pairs.question: What is the impact if a faculty member with sponsored users allows their account to expire?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are a faculty member with sponsored users and you allow your account to expire, your account as well as the account of all those users you sponsor will be deactivated. Any job submitted by you or your sponsored users will be paused, and your research group will lose access to their files until the sponsor account is reactivated.

qa_pairs.question: What should a faculty member do if their account or their sponsored users' accounts are no longer needed?
qa_pairs.answer: If your account or those of your sponsored users are no longer needed, you can let them expire.

qa_pairs.question: What should a faculty member do if their account or their sponsored users' accounts are still needed but their situation has changed?
qa_pairs.answer: If your account or those of your sponsored users are still needed, you must request that they be renewed or apply for a new role if your situation or that of your sponsored user(s) has changed.
